channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly tipping from the men from nr1 !!!!! Mark, 1st May
item.description:
The Men from number one keep dumping his rubbish on the street after the fire

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Fassett Square, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed vehicle on the road and being driven daily, 1st May
item.description:
Car has been parked up on the public road without being taxed since 24th April 2026 but the car is being used and is being driven daily every day since the 24th April and is always parked in the same place outside number 14 Stevedale Road in Welling Kent in front of the house on the public road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stevedale Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Vehicles for sale on the public road

item.title: Permit signs missing, 30th April
item.description:
High street outside council offices Several poles with missing or loose signs
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, High Street, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping Charnwood Road, 30th April
item.description:
Fridge and other items dumped at Charnwood House on Northwold Estate

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charnwood Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish left on street, 30th April
item.description:
Rubbish dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brook Lane, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic light danger, 30th April
item.description:
The crossing flashes an amber light for cars and the green man flashes at the same time so both pedestrians and cars go. It’s incredibly dangerous as it’s a busy road at times and people don’t look where they are going and cars assume no one is going to cross. I have seen many people almost get run over and traffic honking. It’s a danger please can it be fixed. Traffic like at the end of park to the entrance of the park by the play park. Or how do I go about getting it fixed?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Outer Circle, Chalk Farm, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavement cleaning and grass cutting, 30th April
item.description:
I recently reported a problem on Monday 27th April about the cleaning of the streets and pavements on Lawn Road, and Copers Cope Road. On Tuesday 28th April I received an email that this was fixed. When I got home in the evening, I have seen that a couple of twigs have been removed, but nothing else was done. The grass is still not cut, the grass verges are still not cleaned, the pavements are not swept. The locations are just outside of Amberley Court and Coper Grange on Lawn Road and Copers Cope Road leading to Park Road. The grass has to be cut, the streets and pavements to be swept and the debris collected, and the grass verges to be cleaned and debris collected.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lawn Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven surface outside St Michaels school / on way to park, 30th April
item.description:
The surfacing outside the gates of St Michael’s school (directly next to St Stevens park) is extremely uneven and children fall over and hurt themselves every day before and after school. This may be due to the large tree roots but the surface needs to be re done. Many parents have spoken to the school about it but they have said its council’s responsibility
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 50, Wrotham Road, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Playground equiptment needs fixing, 30th April
item.description:
Equipment has been broken for months and children still play on it. Please can this be sorted asap. Also the roundabout in the baby area is very stiff and needs fixing.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wrotham Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rosendale Road SE24 at the side of 119 Norwood, 30th April
item.description:
Rosendale Road SE24 at the side of 119 Norwood Road (Westmoreland Lodge) plastic yellow grit bin graffiti tagged about twenty or so yards from Norwood Road junction. Please forward fixmystreet.com link to your contractor for reporting pin &, picture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 282, Rosendale Road, Tulse Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped bin on Roslin Way between numbers 8 and 10, 30th April
item.description:
The bin has been on the pavement for about a week and has been filled up with rubbish then emptied. It remains on the pavement and will fill up again unless it is taken away.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Roslin Way, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Coloured graffiti tags on wall and gas box, 30th April
item.description:
These graffiti tags are visible from Camberwell New Road crossing and Warham Street. In recent weeks more tags have been popping up and leaving them there seems to encourage others to vandalise the wall. It’s an eyesore and makes the street look messy.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Warham Street, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Safety concern – bee swarm at Danson Park, 30th April
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, Today, while walking in Danson Park with my child, we noticed what appears to be a swarm of bees clustered on a tree branch. The swarm is hanging quite low and looks like a large cone-shaped mass. This is located near the children’s playground, opposite Old English Park. As it is within easy reach, there is a risk that a small child may try to touch it. For safety reasons, I would kindly ask you to arrange for this to be safely assessed and, if necessary, removed or relocated. I have attached a photo for your reference. Thank you for your attention. Kind regards, Natalja

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Park, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Denmark Hill SE5 north bound side footpath (so, 30th April
item.description:
Denmark Hill SE5 north bound side footpath (so Lambeth side) outside house number 164a Lambeth black square bin graffiti tagged. Please forward fixmystreet.com link to your contractor for reporting pin (approx.) &, picture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-36, Denmark Hill, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Collapsing manhole. Dropped circa 100mm +. Very dangerous as on the downhill side, 30th April
item.description:
Collapsing manhole dropped circa 100mm+. Downhill side very dangerous as sudden drop for two wheel vehicles

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 7, Cameron Road, Hayes, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: NF graffitied on side of garage, 30th April
item.description:
NF logo graffitied on side of garage at Orwell Court. The National Front were a racist, far right group. This type of graffiti is offensive and has no place in Hackney or anywhere else for that matter.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Broadway Market Mews, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light out between 11 and 13 Crossway , Petts Wood, 30th April
item.description:
This street light has been out for quite a while and it makes it dark walking at night.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Crossway, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parkside Barnehurst road, 30th April
item.description:
The recent work that was done at Parkside has dipped again which requires urgent attention and arrange the works to be rescheduled and completed correctly and ensure that the road suitable for a users
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Parkside Avenue, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 30th April
item.description:
After nearly a weeks respite, we have the ominous black bags again, fly tipped in their usual spot. Thanks.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Talbot Road, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned caravan causing obstruction and access issues, 30th April
item.description:
A large abandoned caravan has been parked outside residential properties on this road for several weeks and appears to have been left unattended. It has no visible registration plate or tax details and is in a deteriorated condition. The caravan is causing a significant obstruction to the road and is preventing refuse trucks from accessing the street properly. This is now affecting waste collection for residents. The size and positioning of the caravan also create visibility and access concerns for other vehicles using the road. The caravan appears neglected and partially damaged, with missing fittings/panels and signs of disrepair. It has remained stationary for an extended period with no indication that it is being used or moved. Please can this be investigated urgently and appropriate action taken to remove the vehicle and restore safe access to the street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Greenbay Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet
